steps.title: Prepare biological safety cabinet by turning...
steps.instruction: Prepare biological safety cabinet by turning on the blower, and sterilizing with the UV light. After 30 minutes, turn off the UV light and wipe down the BSC with 70% ethanol.

steps.title: Bring in insect cadavers from field...
steps.instruction: Bring in insect cadavers from field collection, gather metadata, and take dissecting scope photos of the cadaver.

steps.title: Microscopically search and document cadaver for...
steps.instruction: Microscopically search and document cadaver for rhizoids, conidiophores, and resting spores.

steps.title: Remove mycosed cadaver from substrate (leaf, bark, rock, etc
steps.instruction: Remove mycosed cadaver from substrate (leaf, bark, rock, etc.). If this is not possible, remove as much substrate as possible surrounding the cadaver.

steps.title: Place insect cadaver in the center...
steps.instruction: Place insect cadaver in the center of a sterile empty petri dish.

steps.title: Place sterile ring of filter paper...
steps.instruction: Place sterile ring of filter paper around the edge of the plate, avoiding contact with cadaver.

steps.title: Use 1000ml pipette tip to add...
steps.instruction: Use 1000ml pipette tip to add water to filter paper, not too much that it starts to pool but enough to coat the paper. Be careful to not allow the water to touch the insect cadaver.

steps.title: Parafilm the plate, be careful not...
steps.instruction: Parafilm the plate, be careful not to jostle it, and keep it in the incubator for around 24 hours.

steps.title: Continue monitoring the dish until spores...
steps.instruction: Continue monitoring the dish until spores are shot out from the cadaver and are visible on the lid of the petri dish.

steps.title: In the BSC, cut a small...
steps.instruction: In the BSC, cut a small square out of a plate of SDAYEYM media with a sterile scalpel.

steps.title: Use a sterile tool to rub...
steps.instruction: Use a sterile tool to rub the media along the lid of the petri dish where there are spores. Do this step quickly, try to keep the plates open as briefly as possible.

steps.title: Place media back onto previous plate...
steps.instruction: Place media back onto previous plate in a new area, sandwiching the spore collecting side between the side you had cut out and the plate media.

steps.title: Keep plate in incubator, it should...
steps.instruction: Keep plate in incubator, it should show signs of growth between 48 and 96 hours.

steps.title: Continue to transfer as needed to maintain culture, approximately monthly
steps.instruction: Continue to transfer as needed to maintain culture, approximately monthly.

steps.title: Before continuing on to DNA extraction,...
steps.instruction: Before continuing on to DNA extraction, transfer one clean plate that has not been touched since the original transfer and input the sample and metadata into ARSEF.

steps.title: When plates have substantial growth along...
steps.instruction: When plates have substantial growth along the surface of the media, they are ready for transfer to liquid culture. Be sure to maintain the solid media plate (and a backup) just in case.

steps.title: Prepare two 150mL flasks
steps.instruction: Prepare two 150mL flasks: add 25mL of Grace’s insect media and 1mL of liquid FBS to each.

steps.title: Cut a square into the culture...
steps.instruction: Cut a square into the culture plate and scrape/peel culture from the media. It is ok to also take some SDAYEYM media, if necessary, but as minimal as possible.

steps.title: Cut the piece of fungus into...
steps.instruction: Cut the piece of fungus into 3-4 smaller pieces, then deposit using sterile tool into the flask of liquid media.

steps.title: Repeat for the other flask
steps.instruction: Repeat for the other flask.

steps.title: Leave both flasks in shaker at...
steps.instruction: Leave both flasks in shaker at room temperature for ~1 week.

steps.title: When liquid culture has developed enough...
steps.instruction: When liquid culture has developed enough to see significant hyphal growth, remove cultures from shaker.

steps.title: Pour both 25mL flasks into one 50mL Falcon tube
steps.instruction: Pour both 25mL flasks into one 50mL Falcon tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 4000g for 15 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 4000g for 15 minutes.

steps.title: Once culture has pelleted at the...
steps.instruction: Once culture has pelleted at the bottom, pour off Grace’s media supernatant.

steps.title: Transfer pellet to 1
steps.instruction: Transfer pellet to 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes, using sterile tool or pipette tip. The pellet should be large enough to fill three or four tubes about halfway.

steps.title: Remove from liquid nitrogen, open lid...
steps.instruction: Remove from liquid nitrogen, open lid of Eppendorf tubes and cover with foil, poking five holes in the foil with sharp forceps.

steps.title: Place onto tube rack and into...
steps.instruction: Place onto tube rack and into lyophilizer according to fungal protocols for 48 hours.

steps.title: Check appearance of tissue after 48...
steps.instruction: Check appearance of tissue after 48 hours, if light in color and dry then remove from lyophilizer and store at -80°C until ready for extraction.

steps.title: Use lyophilized material for high molecular...
steps.instruction: Use lyophilized material for high molecular weight gDNA extraction protocol of choice.

steps.title: Use one tube for extraction, maintaining...
steps.instruction: Use one tube for extraction, maintaining a backup of the material in the -80°C freezer.

steps.title: Once DNA has been extracted and...
steps.instruction: Once DNA has been extracted and stored at 4°C overnight, relax it once daily for three days.

steps.title: Pipette up and down once VERY...
steps.instruction: Pipette up and down once VERY SLOWLY using a wide bore 1000uL tip.

steps.title: Incubate DNA at 37°C for one hour
steps.instruction: Incubate DNA at 37°C for one hour.

steps.title: Repeat step 2
steps.instruction: Repeat step 2.

steps.title: Return to 4°C until the next...
steps.instruction: Return to 4°C until the next day, then repeat steps 2-5.

steps.title: After three days, run Broad Range...
steps.instruction: After three days, run Broad Range Qubit according to protocols with 2ul of sample each from the top, middle and bottom of Eppendorf tube.

steps.title: After Qubit, calculate how much sample...
steps.instruction: After Qubit, calculate how much sample is necessary to follow sequencing facility guidelines and transfer that amount to a screw-cap sequencing tube for transport.

steps.title: Double seal tubes in a Ziploc...
steps.instruction: Double seal tubes in a Ziploc bag inside of an empty pipette tip box.

steps.title: Include an ice pack and scrap...
steps.instruction: Include an ice pack and scrap paper to keep sample from jostling during transit.

steps.title: Print shipping label and cover in...
steps.instruction: Print shipping label and cover in clear packing tape on top of box, deliver to FedEx.

steps.title: Metadata includes location coordinates, date, host ID, substrate, and description
steps.instruction: Metadata includes location coordinates, date, host ID, substrate, and description.

steps.title: The spore pattern will often be...
steps.instruction: The spore pattern will often be directly above where the cadaver is on the plate, but look at the plate under a microscope first to confirm.

steps.title: If contamination appears, remove a small...
steps.instruction: If contamination appears, remove a small clean section of culture and place culture side down onto a plate of PDA media.

steps.title: Entomophthorales should be “unhappy” on this...
steps.instruction: Entomophthorales should be “unhappy” on this media and shoot spores toward the lid within a few days.

steps.title: Repeat steps 10-13 of the Culturing...
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 10-13 of the Culturing section using the PDA plate lid, transferring back onto SDAYEYM media.

steps.title: Drop Eppendorf tubes into liquid nitrogen...
steps.instruction: Drop Eppendorf tubes into liquid nitrogen to freeze instantly and maintain gDNA quality.

steps.title: Instructions are listed in manual on...
steps.instruction: Instructions are listed in manual on top of machine but should run under “AUTO” option.

steps.title: Our lab follows the Lovett lab...
steps.instruction: Our lab follows the Lovett lab CTAB protocol along with NEB Monarch HMW gDNA Extraction Kit.

steps.title: This step is to test that...
steps.instruction: This step is to test that the DNA is fully relaxed evenly throughout the tube.
